rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Dated parenthetical markers (2026-05-26 / 2026-05-27) appear only below the maintainer cut; body region is clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Register match.
Rough-cut drafts for articles.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Articles-only rule; this is a curriculum exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit original when paraphrasing named technique.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Boris Cherny worktree framing credited; lives in maintainer block, which is fine for a non-named-move mechanic.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Over-hedge detector.
Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Reference-artefact authoring rule; not an exercise body concern.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No practitioner attribution in body; Boris Cherny credit is maintainer-only.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps punch; carve-out lives downstream.
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project strategy docs, NOT student-facing curriculum body.
Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: STRIDE / ADR / test-strategy skill / user scope all land operationally inside M3; no term lifted ahead of its arc position.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit closer when trimming body-prose forcing function.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No dangling pronouns or bypass-the-agent punchlines; closer narrates state, no orphaned demonstratives.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Past tense for exercise recap sections — heading and body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Heading is **What happened:**; recap body uses past tense (forked, opened, confirmed); stable-state lines stay present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: "firing the fork" / "kicks off a long-running prompt" describe triggering a prompt, not commanding the agent; peer-flat throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No corrective-gap framing, no ability-tier pegging; "the move you're installing" is aspirational and tier-neutral.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: "sibling worktree" earned with one-breath primer at line 7; STRIDE/ADR/user scope land operationally in M3 per the structural carve-out.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Agents 101 default).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 audience is software engineers, not the SVP-marketing register the Agents 101 default governs; earn-every-term (rule 2) carries the load here.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Neither "memory" nor "context" used in body; the worktree text says "working state" / "git history" with no conflation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Recovery / failure branches live only in the maintainer Push-back moves; body stays forward-moving.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Fork/orient prompts specify the output that matters (worktree path, four confirm lines) without over-shaping structure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module. Compound-shape varies per module — never repeat same Debrief.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Module opener; no Debrief / compound-close beat in this exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle another module's voice.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Voice matches M3 multi-session/security framing; no borrowed M1-joy or M5-long-running mood.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't ask student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
Simulation personas never alone.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No simulation in this file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No "What research says" callout boxes
Student moves stay in conversation with agent.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forcing function is the two fenced prompts (fork via Claude, orient via Claude); the only out-of-chat act is arranging windows, a setup mechanic, not a primitive substituting for the agent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body names the personal layer explicitly as `CLAUDE.local.md`; no bare unqualified `CLAUDE.md` in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No em-dashes in body; semicolons/commas used. Line 7 is long but splits into short clauses on read.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Headers are the Session widget label and the canonical **What happened:** recap; no non-canonical do-section carries a noun-phrase header.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use target's title, never filename.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 7 link text is the title "Multi-session and Git survival guide"; `CLAUDE.local.md` code-span is a student-owned file, not a cross-referenced curriculum surface.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: "test-strategy skill" disambiguated by "installs to user scope"; this is M3, skill-as-primitive already met upstream, so no first-authored-use collision needs an inline split here.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: "Claude" uses are product/UI/invocation ("Claude Code session", "Claude reads `<repo-name>`", "Claude reports the path"); the file is session/worktree mechanics, not autonomous-agent work, so there is no mislabeled-agency 10:0 signal.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: "from here" is session-relative; "long-running prompt" is a mechanic; no tomorrow/overnight/morning anchors.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in flow.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No pedagogy-aware header or contemplative-pause prefix; transitions use command verbs ("Start a new...", "Before firing the fork, decide...").

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No meta-ask when student has already decided.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Orient prompt asks Claude to confirm worktree state, not to ask the student what they already decided.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: `.md` is default file type for student artifacts across curriculum.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No text-artifact save prompt in this exercise; nothing names a file extension to default.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cut NVA: true but not moving student forward.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Each body line advances the setup (define worktree, base branch, fork, orient, arrange windows, recap); no implementation-detail or maintainer-reasoning leak in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body "read it" / "look at it" / "notice X" after chat-print = dead weight.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: After Claude reports the path / four lines, body moves to the next action (open second session, arrange windows), not a "read it" narration.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forward-callout closer: student as grammatical subject.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forward line "The move you're installing this module... the other is where you work" keeps "you" as actor; no role-gating or training-as-subject.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Operational reminders live at moment of use, not only where introduced.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Module opener restates the operational state it needs (two windows, one session per worktree, rename shortcuts) at point of use rather than relying on prework recall.
